translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the days when the judges judged, there was a famine in the land. A certain man of Bethlehem Judah went to live in the country of Moab, he, and his wife, and his two sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The name of the man was Elimelech, and the name of his wife Naomi. The names of his two sons were Mahlon and Chilion, Ephrathites of Bethlehem Judah. They came into the country of Moab, and lived there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elimelech, Naomi’s husband, died; and she was left with her two sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took for themselves wives of the women of Moab. The name of the one was Orpah, and the name of the other was Ruth. They lived there about ten years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mahlon and Chilion both died, and the woman was bereaved of her two children and of her husband.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she arose with her daughters-in-law, that she might return from the country of Moab; for she had heard in the country of Moab how Yahweh  had visited his people in giving them bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She went out of the place where she was, and her two daughters-in-law with her. They went on the way to return to the land of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naomi said to her two daughters-in-law, “Go, return each of you to her mother’s house. May Yahweh deal kindly with you, as you have dealt with the dead, and with me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: May Yahweh grant you that you may find rest, each of you in the house of her husband.” Then she kissed them, and they lifted up their voices, and wept.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to her, “No, but we will return with you to your people.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naomi said, “Go back, my daughters. Why do you want to go with me? Do I still have sons in my womb, that they may be your husbands?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go back, my daughters, go your way; for I am too old to have a husband. If I should say, ‘I have hope,’ if I should even have a husband tonight, and should also bear sons;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: would you then wait until they were grown? Would you then refrain from having husbands? No, my daughters, for it grieves me seriously for your sakes, for Yahweh’s hand has gone out against me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They lifted up their voices, and wept again; then Orpah kissed her mother-in-law, but Ruth joined with her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said, “Behold,  your sister-in-law has gone back to her people, and to her god. Follow your sister-in-law.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ruth said, “Don’t urge me to leave you, and to return from following you, for where you go, I will go; and where you stay, I will stay. Your people will be my people, and your God  my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Where you die, I will die, and there I will be buried. May Yahweh do so to me, and more also, if anything but death parts you and me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she saw that she was determined to go with her, she stopped urging her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they both went until they came to Bethlehem. When they had come to Bethlehem, all the city was excited about them, and they asked, “Is this Naomi?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said to them, “Don’t call me Naomi.  Call me Mara;  for the Almighty has dealt very bitterly with me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I went out full, and Yahweh has brought me home again empty. Why do you call me Naomi, since Yahweh has testified against me, and the Almighty has afflicted me?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Naomi returned, and Ruth the Moabitess, her daughter-in-law, with her, who returned out of the country of Moab. They came to Bethlehem in the beginning of barley harvest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naomi had a relative of her husband’s, a mighty man of wealth, of the family of Elimelech, and his name was Boaz.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ruth the Moabitess said to Naomi, “Let me now go to the field, and glean among the ears of grain after him in whose sight I find favor.” She said to her, “Go, my daughter.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She went, and came and gleaned in the field after the reapers; and she happened to come to the portion of the field belonging to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, Boaz came from Bethlehem, and said to the reapers, “May Yahweh be with you.” They answered him, “May Yahweh bless you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Boaz said to his servant who was set over the reapers, “Whose young lady is this?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The servant who was set over the reapers answered, “It is the Moabite lady who came back with Naomi out of the country of Moab.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said, ‘Please let me glean and gather after the reapers among the sheaves.’ So she came, and has continued even from the morning until now, except that she rested a little in the house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Boaz said to Ruth, “Listen, my daughter. Don’t go to glean in another field, and don’t go from here, but stay here close to my maidens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your eyes be on the field that they reap, and go after them. Haven’t I commanded the young men not to touch you? When you are thirsty, go to the vessels, and drink from that which the young men have drawn.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she fell on her face, and bowed herself to the ground, and said to him, “Why have I found favor in your sight, that you should take knowledge of me, since I am a foreigner?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Boaz answered her, “I have been fully told about all that you have done to your mother-in-law since the death of your husband, and how you have left your father and your mother, and the land of your birth, and have come to a people that you didn’t know before.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: May Yahweh repay your work, and a full reward be given to you from Yahweh, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come to take refuge.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she said, “Let me find favor in your sight, my lord, because you have comforted me, and because you have spoken kindly to your servant, though I am not as one of your servants.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At meal time Boaz said to her, “Come here, and eat some bread, and dip your morsel in the vinegar.” She sat beside the reapers, and they passed her parched grain, and she ate, and was satisfied, and left some of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she had risen up to glean, Boaz commanded his young men, saying, “Let her glean even among the sheaves, and don’t reproach her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also pull out some for her from the bundles, and leave it. Let her glean, and don’t rebuke her.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So she gleaned in the field until evening; and she beat out that which she had gleaned, and it was about an ephah  of barley.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She took it up, and went into the city. Then her mother-in-law saw what she had gleaned; and she brought out and gave to her that which she had left after she had enough.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her mother-in-law said to her, “Where have you gleaned today? Where have you worked? Blessed be he who noticed you.” She told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked, “The man’s name with whom I worked today is Boaz.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naomi said to her daughter-in-law, “May he be blessed by Yahweh, who has not left off his kindness to the living and to the dead.” Naomi said to her, “The man is a close relative to us, one of our near kinsmen.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ruth the Moabitess said, “Yes, he said to me, ‘You shall stay close to my young men, until they have finished all my harvest.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naomi said to Ruth her daughter-in-law, “It is good, my daughter, that you go out with his maidens, and that they not meet you in any other field.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So she stayed close to the maidens of Boaz, to glean to the end of barley harvest and of wheat harvest; and she lived with her mother-in-law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naomi her mother-in-law said to her, “My daughter, shall I not seek rest for you, that it may be well with you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now isn’t Boaz our kinsman, with whose maidens you were? Behold, he will be winnowing barley tonight on the threshing floor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore wash yourself, anoint yourself, get dressed, and go down to the threshing floor, but don’t make yourself known to the man until he has finished eating and drinking.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be, when he lies down, that you shall note the place where he is lying. Then you shall go in, uncover his feet, and lay down. Then he will tell you what to do.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said to her, “All that you say, I will do.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She went down to the threshing floor, and did everything that her mother-in-law told her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Boaz had eaten and drunk, and his heart was merry, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of grain. She came softly, uncovered his feet, and laid down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At midnight, the man was startled and turned himself; and behold, a woman lay at his feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Who are you?” She answered, “I am Ruth your servant. Therefore spread the corner of your garment over your servant; for you are a near kinsman.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “You are blessed by Yahweh, my daughter. You have shown more kindness in the latter end than at the beginning, because you didn’t follow young men, whether poor or rich.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now, my daughter, don’t be afraid. I will do to you all that you say; for all the city of my people knows that you are a worthy woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now it is true that I am a near kinsman. However, there is a kinsman nearer than I.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Stay this night, and in the morning, if he will perform for you the part of a kinsman, good. Let him do the kinsman’s duty. But if he will not do the duty of a kinsman for you, then I will do the duty of a kinsman for you, as Yahweh lives. Lie down until the morning.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She lay at his feet until the morning, then she rose up before one could discern another. For he said, “Let it not be known that the woman came to the threshing floor.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Bring the mantle that is on you, and hold it.” She held it; and he measured six measures of barley, and laid it on her; then he went into the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she came to her mother-in-law, she said, “How did it go, my daughter?” She told her all that the man had done for her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said, “He gave me these six measures of barley; for he said, ‘Don’t go empty to your mother-in-law.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she said, “Wait, my daughter, until you know what will happen; for the man will not rest until he has settled this today.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Boaz went up to the gate, and sat down there. Behold, the near kinsman of whom Boaz spoke came by. He said to him, “Come over here, friend, and sit down!” He turned aside, and sat down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took ten men of the elders of the city, and said, “Sit down here,” and they sat down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the near kinsman, “Naomi, who has come back out of the country of Moab, is selling the parcel of land, which was our brother Elimelech’s.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I thought I should tell you, saying, ‘Buy it before those who sit here, and before the elders of my people.’ If you will redeem it, redeem it; but if you will not redeem it, then tell me, that I may know. For there is no one to redeem it besides you; and I am after you.” He said, “I will redeem it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Boaz said, “On the day you buy the field from the hand of Naomi, you must buy it also from Ruth the Moabitess, the wife of the dead, to raise up the name of the dead on his inheritance.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The near kinsman said, “I can’t redeem it for myself, lest I endanger my own inheritance. Take my right of redemption for yourself; for I can’t redeem it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now this was the custom in former time in Israel concerning redeeming and concerning exchanging, to confirm all things: a man took off his shoe, and gave it to his neighbor; and this was the way of legalizing transactions in Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the near kinsman said to Boaz, “Buy it for yourself,” then he took off his shoe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Boaz said to the elders, and to all the people, “You are witnesses today, that I have bought all that was Elimelech’s, and all that was Chilion’s and Mahlon’s, from the hand of Naomi.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Ruth the Moabitess, the wife of Mahlon, I have purchased to be my wife, to raise up the name of the dead on his inheritance, that the name of the dead may not be cut off from among his brothers, and from the gate of his place. You are witnesses today.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people who were in the gate, and the elders, said, “We are witnesses. May Yahweh make the woman who has come into your house like Rachel and like Leah, which both built the house of Israel; and treat you worthily in Ephrathah, and be famous in Bethlehem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your house be like the house of Perez, whom Tamar bore to Judah, of the offspring  which Yahweh will give you by this young woman.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Boaz took Ruth, and she became his wife; and he went in to her, and Yahweh enabled her to conceive, and she bore a son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The women said to Naomi, “Blessed be Yahweh, who has not left you today without a near kinsman. Let his name be famous in Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall be to you a restorer of life, and sustain you in your old age, for your daughter-in-law, who loves you, who is better to you than seven sons, has given birth to him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naomi took the child, and laid him in her bosom, and became nurse to it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The women, her neighbors, gave him a name, saying, “A son is born to Naomi”. They named him Obed. He is the father of Jesse, the father of David.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now this is the history of the generations of Perez: Perez became the father of Hezron,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Hezron became the father of Ram, and Ram became the father of Amminadab,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Amminadab became the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon became the father of Salmon,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Salmon became the father of Boaz, and Boaz became the father of Obed,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Obed became the father of Jesse, and Jesse became the father of David.
